name: Marketing / Creative Agency
description: Retainers and project work — a Campaigns object for channels, budgets and deliverables, plus a new-business pipeline that runs from brief through pitch to a booked kickoff.
ai_context: You are a CRM assistant for a marketing and creative agency that sells monthly retainers and one-off projects. A contact is a client-side marketer, founder or procurement lead; a deal is one piece of new business — a retainer or a project — and the Campaign object is the work itself once it is sold, carrying channel, budget, dates, KPI and deliverables. Never invent campaign results, media spend, performance numbers, rate cards or client names for case studies; if a figure is not on the record, say it is not recorded.

automation_rules.name: Respond to a new brief within a day
automation_rules.description: Agency briefs usually go to three or four shops at once. Speed of first response is most of the advantage, so this books the callback the moment a contact lands.

params.title: Call {{contact.first_name}} about their brief and book a discovery call
New enquiry to triage
params.body: Capture before the discovery call: what they are actually trying to sell, the budget band, the deadline behind the brief, who else is pitching, and whether there is an incumbent agency.

automation_rules.name: Capture pitch feedback while it is still fresh
automation_rules.description: Fires whenever a deal changes stage so the reason it moved — or the feedback that stalled it — gets written down instead of living in someone's head.

params.title: Log the feedback and confirm the next step on {{deal.title}}
Stage change on {{deal.title}}
params.body: What did the client respond to, what did they push back on, and what is the committed next step and date? If we lost, record whether it was price, chemistry, credentials or timing.

kb_templates.company:
## About us

We are a [full-service / performance / brand / creative] agency in [location], working mostly with [types of client] in [verticals]. Replace this with what you are genuinely known for.

## The basics

- Team of [size]: [strategy, creative, media, account roles]
- Typical client size and spend: [range]
- Minimum engagement: [retainer floor or project minimum]
- Awards, accreditations, platform partnerships: [list]
kb_templates.products:
## How we sell work

- **Monthly retainer** — ongoing strategy, production and reporting. From [amount] per month, [notice period] notice.
- **Project / one-off** — a defined deliverable with a fixed fee: [rebrand, website, launch campaign].
- **Retainer + media** — retainer fee plus [x]% of managed media spend.
- **Audit / consulting** — a paid diagnostic, [amount], often the way a retainer starts.

## Services

[Paid search, paid social, SEO, content, email/CRM, brand and creative, PR, web build] — cut this list to what you actually staff.

## Rate card

Blended day rate [amount]. Out of scope work is quoted before it starts, never absorbed.
kb_templates.playbook:
## Qualifying a brief

1. What is the business outcome, not the deliverable? "We need a website" is rarely the problem.
2. Budget band — ask directly. If they will not say, offer ranges and watch which one they react to.
3. The deadline and what is driving it: a funding round, a season, a product launch.
4. Who signs, and who else is pitching.
5. Is there an incumbent, and what specifically is not working?

## Pitching

- Pitch the thinking, not the deck. One idea done properly beats five sketched.
- Never present work without presenting the strategy that produced it.
- Free speculative creative devalues the whole category — offer a paid discovery instead.

## Retainer health

Review scope every quarter. Silent scope creep is how a profitable retainer becomes a loss-making one without anyone noticing.
kb_templates.process:
## From brief to kickoff

1. **New Enquiry** — brief logged, first response within a day.
2. **Discovery Call** — business outcome, budget band, deadline and decision makers established.
3. **Chemistry & Scoping** — the team they would actually work with meets them; scope and channel mix drafted.
4. **Proposal / Pitch** — strategy, scope, deliverables, team and fee presented.
5. **Negotiation** — scope and fee shaped to fit the budget. Adjust the scope, not the rate.
6. **Contract Sent** — MSA or retainer agreement and schedule of work issued for signature.
7. **Won — Kickoff Booked** — signed, kickoff in the diary. Create the Campaign records with channel, budget, KPI and dates.

Anything that dies goes to **Lost** with a reason: price, chemistry, credentials, timing or no decision.
kb_templates.competitors:
## Why clients choose us

Replace these with your real differentiators — the ones a client has actually said back to you.

- **Against the big networks** — they pitch the senior team and staff the junior one. Name who works on the account and keep it true.
- **Against freelancers and small shops** — we cover [range of channels] under one strategy, with cover when someone is away.
- **Against in-housing** — we bring pattern recognition across [number] accounts, and no recruitment lead time.

The strongest proof is a named client, a starting position, and a number. Keep three of those ready.
